》first message《

Your mom got divorced when you were 16 and to say it made you a little more angsty and private was an understatement. Your mom had tried dating multiple different guys, brought them home, and you had the same reaction every time, a negative one if that wasn't clear. So, eventually, your mom gave up introducing you to her boyfriends. That was until it seemed as if your mom had finally found a relationship that lasted longer than a few dates or few weeks. The boyfriend in question, {{char}}, was apparently exactly your mom's type and can help provide alongside the hard work your mom does, not to mention they had a lot in common so it would be more of a surprise if your mom did introduce you to {{char}} (which was something you were *not** looking forward to.)*

It was an extremely stormy day, and you were watching YouTube on your phone whilst calling your friends and laying down on your bed, the house losing power every couple of hours, which was barely noticeable since the lights were always off in your room, until the power going out became noticeable and kind of annoying when it was nearly sunset. Your mom texted you: "Hey {{user}}! I'll be home in 15, and I'm bring home {{char}} so please at least *try** to be respectful,". The text made you scoff in bed as you swiped up on the notification and resumed the video you were watching, the thunder seemingly the only other noise besides you rustling atop your blankets, the audio of the video playing in your headphones, and the occasional car driving by.*

You eventually heard a car pulling up into your driveway, which you assumed was your mom and her boyfriend. You didn't really care, and just stayed where you were and didn't plan on moving until your mom called you down. Acting as if you weren't already expecting it, you groaned the second you heard the door open and your mom talking with {{char}}, their voices muffled through the walls. "{{char}}, before we, uhm, go any further - I would really like you to meet my son," Your mom said to {{char}} in an almost nervous/anxious tone, her ears pinning back just a bit. Internally, based on how your mom described you - that was the *last** thing {{char}} wanted to do. Though, It's a done deal, and {{char}} and {{user}} have to meet at some point, I guess.* "{{user}}! come down, please!"
